name: Perspective

aliases.name: View
aliases.comment:
The standard TEA view function
as a type.

values.name: elim
values.comment:
Eliminate a Result
(bundled here since its common with above functions)
values.type: (a -> b) -> (c -> b) -> Result a c -> b

values.name: empty
values.comment: Fully generic View, fits in everywhere

values.name: failModel
values.comment: Transform a View such that, passing an `Err` blanks the view.
values.type: View a b -> View (Result c a) (Result c b)

values.name: failMsg
values.type: View a b -> View (Result a c) (Result b c)

values.name: first
values.comment:
Pass a value through to the msg,
on the right hand side of a tuple
values.type: View a b -> View ( a, c ) ( b, c )

values.name: map
values.comment:
One usage of `map` not
to overlook, embeding a sub View

```
type Msg = Sub SubMsg

type SubMsg = Inc | Dec

type alias Model = { sub : Int }

subView : Int -> Html SubMsg

view : Model -> Html Msg
view = div []
  [ h1 [] [text "Foo"]
  , map .sub Sub subView ]
```
values.type: (a -> b) -> (c -> d) -> View b c -> View a d

values.name: mapModel
values.type: (a -> b) -> View b msg -> View a msg

values.name: mapMsg
values.type: (b -> c) -> View a b -> View a c

values.name: second
values.comment:
Pass a value through to the msg,
on the left hand side of a tuple
values.type: View a b -> View ( c, a ) ( c, b )

values.name: sproodle
values.comment:
Handle Html that fires functions.
(Haskell calls this uncurry',
there is no way I'm calling this thing uncurry,
its sproodle)
values.type: View a (b -> c) -> View ( a, b ) c
